steps.text: Set up the retort stand and clamp.
Place 25 mL of 0.1 M HCl in the conical flask.
Add 2-3 drops of phenolphthalein indicator (stays colourless in acid).
Fill the burette with 0.1 M NaOH.
Open the stopcock and titrate. Watch pH rise and the sharp jump at the equivalence point.
At the equivalence point moles of acid equal moles of base. The indicator turns permanent pink just past pH 8.2.
